# @andes/floating-menu

Floating Menu is essentially a card that contains lists with actionable or selectable rows which overlaps or "floats above" the content of an UI.

The UI specs are defined in _[Andes UI](http://andes.adminml.com) > [Floatingmenu](https://andes.mercadolibre.com/924dc6ec5/p/49f28b-floating-menu/b/26f535)_.

## How to use
### Installation

> Follow the [general installation guide](../../docs/installation.md).

```bash
npm install --save @andes/floating-menu
```

#### Important notes
If you're using the `search` variant of FloatingMenu, you'll also need to install `@andes/form`, which will provide styling for this use case.

### Styles

> Follow the [general styles usage guide](../../docs/styles-usage.md) for configuration and how-tos.

Import the components in your bundle:

```scss
@import '@andes/floating-menu/index';
```

>  Or you can import `@andes/floating-menu/base` and only the styles that you need.

In case of using `multiple` prop you need to import multiple styles

```scss
@import '@andes/list/index';
@import '@andes/floating-menu/lib/styles/multiple';
```

> :warning: Due to Checkbox and List variables being used in FloatingMenu styles, you need to import them beforehand

If you're using the `search` variant, you'll also need to import the Form and Searchbox component styles, if you weren't already:

```scss
@import '@andes/form/base';
@import '@andes/floating-menu/lib/styles/search';
```

### Scripts

> Follow the [general scripts usage guide](../../docs/scripts-usage.md) for configuration and how-tos.

Import the component in your page:

```jsx
import { FloatingMenu, FloatingMenuItem, FloatingMenuGroup } from '@andes/floating-menu';
```

## Examples

For full demo and configuration options, check out the [story for Floating Menu](https://andes.adminml.com/web/?path=/story/floating-menu--andes-floating-menu).

### Basic Example
The basic example uses actionable rows, the `onChange` callback returns the event and the value of the row clicked but does not select it, it requires a further action when a row is clicked.

```jsx
<FloatingMenu id="example-with-html-button" onChange={onChangeHandler} srLabel="Basic example" trigger={<button>Trigger</button>}>
  <FloatingMenuItem value="1" title="Item 1" />
  <FloatingMenuItem value="2" title="Item 2" />
  <FloatingMenuItem value="3" title="Item 3" />
</FloatingMenu>
```

### Basic Example with groups

```jsx
<FloatingMenu id="example-with-html-button" onChange={onChangeHandler} srLabel="Group example" trigger={<button>Trigger</button>}>
  <FloatingMenuGroup label="Tarjetas de crédito">
    <FloatingMenuItem value="visa" title="Visa" />
    <FloatingMenuItem value="mastercard" title="Mastercard" />
    <FloatingMenuItem value="amex" title="American Express" />
  </FloatingMenuGroup>
  <FloatingMenuGroup label="Efectivo en redes de pago">
    <FloatingMenuItem value="rapipado" title="RapiPago" />
    <FloatingMenuItem value="pagofacil" title="PagoFacil" />
  </FloatingMenuGroup>
</FloatingMenu>
```

### Select
The select type uses selectable rows, the `onChange` callback returns the event and the value of the row clicked. Also, the row will be shown as selected.

```jsx
<FloatingMenu type="select" id="example-with-html-button" onChange={onChangeHandler} srLabel="Select example" trigger={<button>Trigger</button>}>
  <FloatingMenuItem value="1" title="Item 1" />
  <FloatingMenuItem value="2" title="Item 2" />
  <FloatingMenuItem value="3" title="Item 3" />
</FloatingMenu>
```

### With andes button or component as trigger
If you want to use a component element as a trigger, ensure you forward the ref to the DOM node

For more information about this case, check out the [component children documentation](https://github.com/atomiks/tippyjs-react#component-children).

```jsx
const Trigger = ({ref, ...props}) => (
  <button ref={ref} {...props}>Trigger</button>
);

<FloatingMenu id="example-with-html-button" srLabel="Component trigger example" trigger={<Trigger />}>
  <FloatingMenuItem value="1" title="Item 1" />
  <FloatingMenuItem value="2" title="Item 2" />
</FloatingMenu>
```

### Shadow DOM 

Usage inside an isolated component with Shadow DOM. See [@andes/shadow-dom](https://github.com/melisource/fury_frontend-andes-ui/blob/master/packages/shadow-dom/README.md) package

```jsx
import { useEnableAndesShadowDOM } from '@andes/shadow-dom';

useEnableAndesShadowDOM();

<FloatingMenu id="example-with-html-button" onChange={onChangeHandler} trigger={<button>Trigger</button>}>
  <FloatingMenuItem value="1" title="Item 1" />
  <FloatingMenuItem value="2" title="Item 2" />
  <FloatingMenuItem value="3" title="Item 3" />
</FloatingMenu>
```

## Props
| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `allowUnselect` | Whether the floating menu should allow unselect the current selected option. This is not valid for `multiple`  | `bool` | `false` |  |
| `bottomContent` | Custom content. [See slot](#slots) | `node` |  | `undefined`|
| `children` | Custom content, should be a collection or a mix of `<FloatingMenuGroup>` and `<FloatingMenuItem>` | `node` |  | ✅ |
| `className` | Custom class | `string` | `''` |  |
| `defaultOpen` | Whether the floating menu should be open or not by default (uncontrolled mode) | `boolean` | `false` |  |
| `defaultValue` | Default selected value for floating menu (uncontrolled mode) | `string \| Array<string>` | `undefined` |  |
| `dialogSrLabel` | <sup>1</sup>Accessible label for the menu dialog | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `id` | Component ID | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `menuMaxHeight` | Floating menu max height. It can be used for large options amount | `number` | `undefined` |  |
| `multiple` | Allows a multiple selection menu (not valid for `type="menu"`) | `bool` | `false` |  |
| `onChange` | Callback executed when an option is selected. When `multiple` is on, values will be an array | `function(event, values)` | `undefined` |  |
| `onClose` | Callback executed when the floating menu is closed | `function` | `undefined` |  |
| `onOpen` | Callback executed when the floating menu is open | `function` | `undefined` |  |
| `open` | Whether the floating menu should be open or not (controlled mode) | `boolean` | `undefined` |  |
| `placement` | It's the position of the menu relative to the trigger. This can be `bottom`, `bottom-start`, `bottom-end`, `top`, `top-start`, `top-end`, `left`, `left-start`, `left-end`, `right`, `right-start`, `right-end` | `string` |  | `bottom` | |
| `search` | Flag that enables/disables the search of floating menu options (not valid for `type="menu"`) | `bool, Object<SearchConfig>` | `false` |  |
| `srLabel` | Label for screen readers | `string` |  | ✅ |
| `topContent` | Custom content. [See slot](#slots) | `node` |  | `undefined`|
| `trigger` | <sup>2</sup>Custom content. Element that triggers the action to open and close the menu. Should be a `<button>`  | `node` |  | ✅ |
| `type` | Defines what type of floating menu should be rendered | `string: 'select' \| 'menu'` | `'menu'` |  |
| `value` | Current selected value for floating menu (controlled mode) | `string \| Array<string>` | `undefined` |  |
| `width` | Width in px (number) or in percentage (string) the menu has | `number \| string` | `280` |  |

#### SearchConfig
```jsx
  {
    defaultValue: string, // default: undefined
    filterCondition: function(searchText, value, dropdownItem), // default: undefined
    onChange: function(value), // default: undefined
    placeholder: string, // default: undefined
  }
```

> 1 - The accessible label for the menu dialog is required when the `type` property is `menu`, when it has the `search` property, or when it has `slots`.

> 2 - If you use the `FloatingMenu` with `type="select"`, the trigger must have an associated label to ensure accessibility.

---

### `<FloatingMenuItem>` extends `<ListItem>` [API](https://github.com/melisource/fury_frontend-andes-ui/tree/master/packages/list#listitem)

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `disabled` | Whether the item should be disabled. | `boolean` | `false` |  |
| `title` | Text to display as primary title. | `string` |  | ✅ |
| `value` | The value of the item that will be passed when selected  | `string \| number` |  | ✅ |

---

### `<FloatingMenuGroup>` extends `<ListGroup>` [API](https://github.com/melisource/fury_frontend-andes-ui/blob/master/packages/list/README.md#listgroup)

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `children` | Custom content. Should be one or a collection of `<FloatingMenuItem>`. | `node` | `undefined` |  |

## Theming

This component supports theming. It allows you to pass a `customTheme` object to it adjusting colors, typography, spacing and styles to align with a brand's visual identity, user preferences or project requirements.

<br />

The `customTheme` object can make use of values ​​referenced in the component or CSS properties for quick customization or make use of style overrides according to `data-attributes` specific to the component for more complex cases. We do not recommend the latter since it will be deprecated in the future.

<br />

### Using Referenced Component Values

The FloatingMenu allows you to modify the following attributes depending on its reference value.

| Sub Attribute | Description           |
| ------------- | --------------------- |
| `fill`        | FloatingMenu fill color.  |
| `text`        | FloatingMenu text color.  |

```jsx
const customTheme = {
  components: {
    floatingMenu: {
      color: {
        fill: fillColorValue,
      },
      textResult: {
        color: {
          text: textColorValue,
        },
      },
    },
  },
};
```

> More states may be added in the future, so we recommend consulting the @andes/themes/meli-themes/ml.json file for more detailed information

#### Example

<img src="resources/floating-menu/docs/FloatingMenu-Example-Reference-Theming.png" alt="Reference Theming FloatingMenu Example" width="720" />


```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { FloatingMenu, FloatingMenuItem } from '@andes/floating-menu';
import { Button } from '@andes/button';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import './styles.scss';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    floatingMenu: {
      color: {
        fill: '#d6eaff',
      },
      textResult: {
        color: {
          text: '#007cff',
        },
      },
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <FloatingMenu
      search
      srLabel="Example"
      trigger={<Button>Open</Button>}
    >
      <FloatingMenuItem key="0" title="Item 1" value="0" />
      <FloatingMenuItem key="1" title="Item 2" value="1" />
    </FloatingMenu>
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/floating-menu/index';
@import '@andes/button/index';
```

> Remember that it is not necessary for the ThemeProvider to be next to the component you want to customize. Ideally it should be at the App level, wrapping your entire page.

### Using CSS Properties

The component allows passing CSS properties following this structure:

```jsx
const customTheme = {
  components: {
    floatingMenu: {
      [cssProperty]: propertyValue,
    },
  },
};
```

> Please note that styles applied in this way will impact all Andes FloatingMenu regardless of their variant. Additionally, they have higher precedence than styles applied using referenced component values.

#### Example

<img src="resources/floating-menu/docs/FloatingMenu-Example-CSS-Properties-Theming.png" alt="CSS Properties Theming FloatingMenu Example" width="720" />


```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { FloatingMenu, FloatingMenuItem } from '@andes/floating-menu';
import { Button } from '@andes/button';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import './styles.scss';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    floatingMenu: {
      fontWeight: 'bold',
      fontStyle: 'italic',
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <FloatingMenu
      search
      srLabel="Example"
      trigger={<Button>Open</Button>}
    >
      <FloatingMenuItem key="0" title="Item 1" value="0" />
      <FloatingMenuItem key="1" title="Item 2" value="1" />
    </FloatingMenu>
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/floating-menu/index';
@import '@andes/button/index';
```

> Remember that it is not necessary for the ThemeProvider to be next to the component you want to customize. Ideally it should be at the App level, wrapping your entire page.

### Using `styleOverrides`

This component has a series of `data-attributes` that serve as a hook to style complex cases:

| data-attribute                       | Description                                                                          |
| ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `data-andes-floating-menu`                | Component tag.                                                                        |
| `data-andes-floating-menu-no-result`          | Text tag.                                                                      |

Taking advantage of the them, you can use an entry called `styleOverrides` where you can add styles according to any `data-attribute`.

#### Example

<img src="resources/floating-menu/docs/FloatingMenu-Example-Style-Overrides-Theming.png" alt="Style Overrides FloatingMenu Example" width="720" />


```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { FloatingMenu, FloatingMenuItem } from '@andes/floating-menu';
import { Button } from '@andes/button';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import './styles.scss';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    floatingMenu: {
      styleOverrides: {
        '&[data-andes-floating-menu]': {
          background: '#d6eaff',
        },
        '[data-andes-floating-menu-no-result]': {
          color: '#007cff',
          fontWeight: 'bold',
        },
      },
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <FloatingMenu
      search
      srLabel="Example"
      trigger={<Button>Open</Button>}
    >
      <FloatingMenuItem key="0" title="Item 1" value="0" />
      <FloatingMenuItem key="1" title="Item 2" value="1" />
    </FloatingMenu>
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/floating-menu/index';
@import '@andes/button/index';
```

> Remember that it is not necessary for the ThemeProvider to be next to the component you want to customize. Ideally it should be at the App level, wrapping your entire page.

## Slots

This component supports the use of slots, providing flexible content areas that can be occupied by other components, thus facilitating the composition of more complex structures. Although React does not have a specific feature called "slots," similar behavior can be achieved using props.

<br />

In Andes, we will have default slots, referred to as children, and named slots that depend on each specific component. This means we can define areas where custom content can be inserted, enhancing the flexibility and reusability of our components.

<br />

### Use of slots

The Floating Menu allows you to modify the following slots.

| Slot name            | Description                                               |
| -------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------- |
| `bottomContent` | Allows to insert content at the bottom of the floating menu content. This slot is ideal for situations where the default list design does not meet the specific needs of the interface. |
| `topContent` | Allows to insert content at the top of the floating menu content. This slot is ideal for situations where the default list design does not meet the specific needs of the interface. |

```jsx
<FloatingMenu
  trigger={<button className="custom-trigger">Floating Menu Trigger</button>}
  bottomContent={'The content of the bottom slot'}
  topContent={'The content of the top slot'}
  srLabel="Slots example"
>
  <FloatingMenuItem key="option1" value="option1" title="Option 1" />
</FloatingMenu>
```

> [!NOTE]  
>
> When search prop is used, the topContent prop is ignored

#### Example

<img src="resources/floating-menu/docs/Floating-menu-Example-Reference-Slot.png" alt="Example of Slots with search in Floating Menu" width="300" />

```jsx
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { Button } from '@andes/button';
import { FloatingMenu, FloatingMenuItem } from '@andes/floating-menu';
import './styles.scss';

const MyComponent = () => {
  const [selectedOptions, setSelectedOptions] = useState([]);

  return (
    <FloatingMenu
      trigger={<Button>Floating Menu Trigger</Button>}
      id="floating-menu-story-id"
      menuMaxHeight={300}
      type="select"
      search
      srLabel="Slots example"
      multiple
      bottomContent={
        <div className="custom-content">
          <Button fullWidth onClick={() => console.log(selectedOptions)}>
            Apply
          </Button>
        </div>
      }
      onChange={(event, values) => setSelectedOptions(values)}
    >
      <FloatingMenuItem key="option1" value="option1" title="Option 1" />
      <FloatingMenuItem key="option2" value="option2" title="Option 2" />
      <FloatingMenuItem key="option3" value="option3" title="Option 3" />
      <FloatingMenuItem key="option4" value="option4" title="Option 4" />
    </FloatingMenu>
  );
};

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/button/index';
@import '@andes/floating-menu/index';

.custom-content {
  padding: 16px;
}
```

#### Example

<img src="resources/floating-menu/docs/Floating-menu-Example-Reference-Slot-2.png" alt="Example of Slots only with bottom slot in Floating Menu" width="300" />

```jsx
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { Button } from '@andes/button';
import { FloatingMenu, FloatingMenuItem } from '@andes/floating-menu';
import './styles.scss';

const MyComponent = () => {
  const [selectedOptions, setSelectedOptions] = useState([]);
  const [selectedOptionsTemp, setSelectedOptionsTemp] = useState([]);
  const [applyClicked, setApplyClicked] = useState(false);
  const [menuOpen, setMenuOpen] = useState(false);

  const handleOpen = () => setMenuOpen(true);

  const handleClose = () => {
    if (applyClicked) {
      setSelectedOptions(selectedOptionsTemp);
    } else {
      setSelectedOptionsTemp(selectedOptions);
    }
    setApplyClicked(false);
    setMenuOpen(false);
  };

  const handleApplyClick = () => {
    setApplyClicked(true);
    setMenuOpen(false);
  };

  const handleChange = (event, values) => {
    setSelectedOptionsTemp(values);
  };

  return (
    <FloatingMenu
      trigger={<Button>Floating Menu Trigger</Button>}
      id="floating-menu-story-id"
      menuMaxHeight={300}
      type="select"
      multiple
      open={menuOpen}
      onOpen={handleOpen}
      onClose={handleClose}
      bottomContent={
        <div className="custom-content">
          <Button fullWidth onClick={handleApplyClick}>
            Apply
          </Button>
        </div>
      }
      onChange={handleChange}
      value={selectedOptionsTemp}
      srLabel="Slots example"
    >
      <FloatingMenuItem key="option1" value="option1" title="Option 1" />
      <FloatingMenuItem key="option2" value="option2" title="Option 2" />
      <FloatingMenuItem key="option3" value="option3" title="Option 3" />
      <FloatingMenuItem key="option4" value="option4" title="Option 4" />
    </FloatingMenu>
  );
};

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/button/index';
@import '@andes/floating-menu/index';

.custom-content {
  padding: 16px;
}
```

#### Example

<img src="resources/floating-menu/docs/Floating-menu-Example-Reference-Slot-3.png" alt="Example of Slots with searchbox, tag choices and group list with options" width="300" />

```jsx
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { SearchBox } from '@andes/search-box';
import { TagChoice } from '@andes/tag';
import { Button } from '@andes/button';
import { FloatingMenu, FloatingMenuItem, FloatingMenuGroup } from '@andes/floating-menu';
import './styles.scss';

const MyComponent = () => {
  return (
    <FloatingMenu
      trigger={<Button>Floating Menu Trigger</Button>}
      id="floating-menu-story-id"
      srLabel="Slots example"
      topContent={
        <div className="fm-slot__top-content">
          <SearchBox placeholder="Search" />
          <ul
            className="fm-slot__top-content-tags"
            aria-label='Filtros de búsqueda'
          >
            <li>
              <TagChoice
                label="All"
                defaultSelected
                size="small"
                className="fm-slot__top-content-tags-tag"
              />
            </li>
            <li>
              <TagChoice
                label="Assets"
                size="small"
                className="fm-slot__top-content-tags-tag"
              />
            </li>
            <li>
              <TagChoice
                label="Plugins"
                size="small"
                className="fm-slot__top-content-tags-tag"
              />
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      }
    >
      <FloatingMenuGroup key="Suggestions" label="Suggestions">
        <FloatingMenuItem
          key="Calendar"
          value="Calendar"
          title="Calendar"
          leftContent={<PlaceholderImage16 />}
        />
        <FloatingMenuItem
          key="Mailbox"
          value="Mailbox"
          title="Mailbox"
          leftContent={<PlaceholderImage16 />}
        />
        <FloatingMenuItem
          key="Gallery"
          value="Gallery"
          title="Gallery"
          leftContent={<PlaceholderImage16 />}
        />
      </FloatingMenuGroup>
      <FloatingMenuGroup key="Settings" label="Settings">
        <FloatingMenuItem
          key="Interface"
          value="Interface"
          title="Interface"
          leftContent={<PlaceholderImage16 />}
        />
        <FloatingMenuItem
          key="Code"
          value="Code"
          title="Code"
          leftContent={<PlaceholderImage16 />}
        />
        <FloatingMenuItem
          key="Task"
          value="Task"
          title="Task"
          leftContent={<PlaceholderImage16 />}
        />
      </FloatingMenuGroup>
    </FloatingMenu>
  );
};

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/tag/index';
@import '@andes/floating-menu/index';

.fm-slot__top-content {
  padding: 16px 16px 4px 16px;
}

.fm-slot__top-content-tags {
  margin-top: 8px;
  display: flex;
  list-style-type: none;
  padding-inline-start: 0;
}

.fm-slot__top-content-tags-tag {
  margin-right: 4px;
}
```

> [!NOTE]  
>
> For this example, we've provided only the necessary markup. Since you are using slots, the accessibility implementation and search logic are your responsibility.
